messages.content: What is the cloud?
Computing services (storage, servers, databases, software) delivered over the internet rather than on a local computer. Examples: Google Drive, iCloud, AWS.
How does solar energy work?
Solar panels contain photovoltaic cells that convert sunlight directly into electricity. When photons from sunlight hit the silicon cells, they knock electrons loose, creating an electric current.
How does a computer processor work?

A CPU (central processing unit) executes instructions by fetching them from memory, decoding what they mean, executing the operation, and writing the result back. Modern CPUs do this billions of times per second.
What is 5G?

The fifth generation of mobile network technology, offering much faster speeds, lower latency, and higher capacity than 4G. It enables faster downloads, better video streaming, and supports IoT devices.
What is blockchain?

A distributed digital ledger that records transactions across many computers. Each block of data is linked to the previous one, making it very difficult to alter without detection. Bitcoin uses blockchain.
What is the internet?
A global network of computers and servers connected by cables, fiber optics, and wireless links, allowing them to communicate and share information using standardized protocols.
What is artificial intelligence?
AI refers to computer systems that can perform tasks that typically require human intelligence — like understanding language, recognizing images, solving problems, and learning from experience.
What is the internet?
A global network of computers and servers connected by cables, fiber optics, and wireless links, allowing them to communicate and share information using standardized protocols.
How does solar energy work?
Solar panels contain photovoltaic cells that convert sunlight directly into electricity. When photons from sunlight hit the silicon cells, they knock electrons loose, creating an electric current.
How does GPS work?
GPS receivers get signals from multiple satellites orbiting Earth. By calculating the time it takes each signal to arrive, the receiver can determine your precise location.
What is machine learning?
A type of AI where systems learn from data to improve their performance, without being explicitly programmed. The more data they see, the better they get.
What is M-Pesa?
A mobile money service launched by Safaricom in Kenya in 2007 that allows users to send, receive, and store money using a mobile phone. It revolutionized financial access in Kenya and Africa.
What is the cloud?
Computing services (storage, servers, databases, software) delivered over the internet rather than on a local computer. Examples: Google Drive, iCloud, AWS.
What is 5G?

Energy from sources that naturally replenish — solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and tidal. Unlike fossil fuels, they don't run out and produce little to no greenhouse gases.

What is renewable energy?
Energy from sources that naturally replenish — solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and tidal. Unlike fossil fuels, they don't run out and produce little to no greenhouse gases.
What is the cloud?
Computing services (storage, servers, databases, software) delivered over the internet rather than on a local computer. Examples: Google Drive, iCloud, AWS.
What is 5G?

The fifth generation of mobile network technology, offering much faster speeds, lower latency, and higher capacity than 4G. It enables faster downloads, better video streaming, and supports IoT devices.
What is M-Pesa?
A mobile money service launched by Safaricom in Kenya in 2007 that allows users to send, receive, and store money using a mobile phone. It revolutionized financial access in Kenya and Africa.
How does GPS work?
GPS receivers get signals from multiple satellites orbiting Earth. By calculating the time it takes each signal to arrive, the receiver can determine your precise location.
